title: Daily Source Digest
note: Daily source-balanced news digest — morning update.

stories.title: The Nigerian army frees 360 abducted people in northeastern Borno state
stories.summary:
The Nigerian army has freed 360 people abducted by Boko Haram in southern Borno state, located in the northeastern part of the country. Military officials confirmed the rescue operation took place on Sunday.

According to reports from Al Jazeera and BBC, the rescued captives include many women and children who were taken from an area near the Cameroon border in March. The group was discovered at a mountain hideout used by the insurgent group.

links.headline: Nigerian forces rescue 360 captives from Boko Haram
links.excerpt: Nigerian forces rescued 360 captives, including women and children from a Boko Haram mountain hideout in Borno State.
links.imageAlt: Nigerian forces rescue 360 captives from Boko Haram

stories.title: Someone’s Trying To Stop The UFC’s White House Fight
stories.summary:
A federal lawsuit filed by two Virginia residents is seeking to halt a planned UFC event on the White House South Lawn. The complaint, submitted Saturday by the Public Integrity Project, challenges President Donald Trump’s plans for his 80th birthday celebration and part of the nation’s 250th anniversary festivities.

The legal challenge alleges that the president did not secure proper approval to host the mixed martial arts show on executive property. Supporters of the administration have characterized the lawsuit as an example of "lawfare" aimed at disrupting the event.

The dispute marks another legal obstacle in the ongoing debates over how the White House commemorates significant national milestones. The outcome could set a precedent for future uses of the South Lawn for high-profile entertainment events.

links.headline: Lawsuit seeks to stop Trump’s planned White House UFC match
links.excerpt: Legal challenge claims US president did not seek proper approval for fighting event to be held on his 80th birthday.
links.imageAlt: Lawsuit seeks to stop Trump’s planned White House UFC match

stories.title: One dead after Palestinian man goes on shooting rampage in central Israel: Police
stories.summary:
A Palestinian man in his 20s opened fire from a vehicle across several towns in central Israel on Sunday, resulting in one death and five injuries. Israeli authorities confirmed that the victim was a 35-year-old Israeli man, while two of the injured individuals sustained severe wounds.

The suspect targeted multiple locations before being shot dead by security forces. Police identified the gunman as holding Israeli citizenship despite his Palestinian background.

links.headline: One dead, five injured after shooting in Israel
links.excerpt: The suspect opened fire from a car in several locations before being shot dead.

stories.title: Over One Million Gather in Madrid to Join Pope Leo XIV for Corpus Domini Mass
stories.summary:
Over one million people gathered in Madrid on Sunday to attend the Corpus Domini Mass presided over by Pope Leo XIV. The event honored Spain's long-standing tradition of religious devotion, with the Pope describing it as a "school of faith" for contemporary society.

The celebration featured a procession across carpets made of flower petals, one of the most iconic expressions of Spanish popular piety. Al Jazeera reported that vast crowds flooded the streets to witness the event, while also noting the Pope's call for unity amidst the massive turnout.

links.headline: People flood Madrid streets to see Pope Leo’s flower-carpeted procession
links.excerpt: Pope Leo XIV hails Spain’s ‘school of faith’ at Madrid Mass, urges unity amid vast Corpus Domini crowds.

stories.title: Israel launches airstrikes on Iran after taking missile fire
stories.summary:
Israel launched airstrikes early Monday targeting central and western Iran in response to missile fire from Tehran, according to the Washington Times. The attacks have raised concerns that the conflict could escalate into a broader regional war across the Middle East.

Al Jazeera reports that tensions escalated further as both nations exchanged threats following Tehran's launch of missiles toward Israel. This retaliation came in direct response to recent Israeli strikes on Beirut.

links.headline: Iran and Israel trade threats after Tehran launches missiles
links.excerpt: Iran and Israel exchanged threats after Tehran launched missiles towards Israel in response to Israeli strikes on Beirut

stories.title: Israel strikes Beirut suburb days after US-brokered truce
stories.summary:
Israel has launched strikes on a suburb of Beirut, marking a significant escalation just days after a US-brokered truce was established in the region. The military operation targets areas within the Lebanese capital, signaling a rapid breakdown of the recent diplomatic efforts aimed at de-escalating tensions between Israel and Hezbollah.

Israeli officials stated that the attacks were ordered as a direct response to Hezbollah firing into Israeli territory. This justification underscores the ongoing cycle of retaliation that has characterized the conflict, despite international attempts to secure a ceasefire.

Al Jazeera reported multiple explosions in Beirut’s southern suburbs following the launch of these new attacks. The network confirmed that Israel is claiming its operations are specifically aimed at targeting Hezbollah positions within the area.

links.headline: Al Jazeera reports from Beirut after Israeli attack
links.excerpt: Multiple explosions have hit Beirut’s southern suburbs as Israel launches new attacks, claiming to target Hezbollah.
links.imageAlt: Al Jazeera reports from Beirut after Israeli attack

stories.title: Spencer Pratt falls behind Nithya Raman in LA mayoral race after latest vote update
stories.summary:
Republican Spencer Pratt has fallen behind progressive Los Angeles City Councilmember Nithya Raman in the city’s mayoral primary election following a significant update to late-arriving ballots. The shift occurred on Sunday night, marking a dramatic turnaround for the former reality television star who had previously held a commanding lead over his political rival.

According to Associated Press vote counts, Raman has overtaken Pratt by more than 3,000 votes, securing an estimated 27.1% of the total ballots cast. This surge places her in second position, effectively threatening Pratt’s chances of advancing to the November general election against incumbent Mayor Karen Bass.

While the race remains officially uncalled due to ongoing vote tabulation, the latest data suggests that Raman is poised to lock up the runoff spot. The development represents a stunning reversal for Pratt, who had trailed by less than nine points before the recent influx of mail-in and provisional ballots tipped the scales in favor of the councilwoman.

links.headline: Spencer Pratt’s Runoff Hopes Take A Hit As Socialist Rival Surges
links.excerpt: Leftist Los Angeles City Councilmember Nithya Raman will advance to the November mayoral election after nearly a week of counting late-arriving ballots, locking Republican Spencer Pratt out of the race. Sunday’s vote drop surged Raman past Pratt for the first time, a stunning turnaround for the leftist who trailed Pratt by more than nine points ...

stories.title: Will robots simply bore us to extinction?
stories.summary:
The Spectator Australia examines the geopolitical tensions involving Russia and Turkey in the Balkans and Caucasus regions. The publication highlights that there are currently 34 disputed territories located within or surrounding Europe, creating a complex landscape of international conflict.

In several instances, multiple nations lay claim to the same territorial patches, intensifying diplomatic friction. The article suggests that both Vladimir Putin and Recep Tayyip Erdogan are engaging in risky strategic maneuvers by leveraging these longstanding disputes for political advantage.

stories.title: Richard Scolyer, acclaimed melanoma researcher who tried experimental treatment on his own brain cancer – obituary
stories.summary:
Richard Scolyer, a distinguished melanoma researcher and former Australian of the Year, has died at age 59 following a diagnosis with inoperable brain cancer. The pathologist was widely recognized for his pioneering contributions to immunotherapy research before facing his own devastating health challenge.

In 2023, Scolyer received the terminal diagnosis and subsequently underwent an experimental treatment regimen targeting his condition. His personal journey became public through an open letter addressed to Australians, in which he shared insights gained from his experience with the disease.

stories.title: Missing Melbourne teacher allegedly drugged and murdered by brother in India
stories.summary:
Police in India have arrested four individuals, including the brother of Sunil Sharma, a Melbourne teacher who went missing in Punjab state on 22 May. Authorities allege that Sharma was drugged and murdered by his sibling during the incident.

The investigation remains ongoing as law enforcement works to establish the full circumstances surrounding the disappearance and subsequent death of the Australian national.

stories.title: Russia won’t give up Armenia without a fight
stories.summary:
The Spectator Australia examines the political landscape in Armenia, highlighting the challenges faced by opposition figures under Russian influence. The publication notes that Samvel Karapetyan, a prominent Russian-Armenian businessman, finds himself effectively sidelined due to house arrest, illustrating the broader difficulty of mounting an electoral challenge against entrenched power structures.

The article suggests that Russia is unlikely to relinquish its hold on Armenia without significant resistance. This perspective frames the ongoing political tensions as part of a larger geopolitical struggle, where external pressures complicate domestic democratic processes and limit the viable options for local leadership contenders.

links.headline: Darren Jones’ Mandy memory failure

stories.title: Hacked, leaked, and held for ransom: the worst breaches of 2026 so far
stories.summary:
The first half of 2026 has seen a series of significant cybersecurity incidents, ranging from large-scale data breaches to attacks on critical infrastructure. Among the most notable events is a massive breach involving data associated with DOGE, which exposed sensitive information on a broad scale.

Critical energy and water systems also fell victim to hacking attempts, raising concerns about national security and public safety. These intrusions highlight vulnerabilities in essential services that rely heavily on digital networks for operation and monitoring.

Additionally, an FBI surveillance system was compromised, marking a high-profile breach of law enforcement technology. This incident underscores the growing sophistication of cyber threats targeting government agencies and their operational capabilities.

links.headline: Hacked, leaked, and held for ransom: the worst breaches of 2026 so far
links.excerpt: From a massive DOGE data breach and the hacking of critical energy and water systems to the hack of an FBI surveillance system, here are the most damaging security incidents and data breaches of 2026.
links.imageAlt: Hacked, leaked, and held for ransom: the worst breaches of 2026 so far

stories.title: Spyro the Dragon returns with a new game after almost two decades
stories.summary:
Spyro the Dragon is returning to video games with a new original title after nearly two decades. The purple dragon, a mascot of 90s PlayStation gaming, was absent from recent nostalgia-driven revivals that have brought other millennial icons back.

The announcement was made during tonight’s Xbox Game Showcase. The upcoming game, titled Spyro: A Realm Beyond, marks the first original title for the character since 2008.

Toys for Bob, a California-based studio known for previous entries in the franchise, is developing the new installment. The developers have stated that the project aims to inspire love, joy, and laughter among players.

A new game starring the purple dragon was announced at tonight’s Xbox Game Showcase – the first original title since 2008. Called Spyro: A Realm Beyond, it is being developed by studi

stories.title: ‘The CGI would have cost millions. I spent $2,000.’ Is Dreams of Violets AI slop – or the future of film-making?
stories.summary:
Ash Koosha’s 75-minute drama Dreams of Violets is set to premiere at the Tribeca film festival in New York next week, marking a notable milestone as the first AI-generated feature to screen at a major international event. The film depicts the brutal crackdown on anti-government protests in Iran earlier this year and was produced in a matter of weeks rather than years.

Koosha claims that traditional CGI for such a project would have cost millions, whereas he completed the production for approximately $2,000 using artificial intelligence tools. He argues that this dramatic reduction in cost and time could fundamentally transform independent filmmaking by lowering barriers to entry.

The film’s selection has sparked debate regarding whether AI-generated cinema represents a legitimate artistic evolution or merely disposable content. As it prepares for its festival debut, Dreams of Violets stands at the center of discussions about the future role of generative technology in professional storytelling.

links.headline: ‘The CGI would have cost millions. I spent $2,000.’ Is Dreams of Violets AI slop – or the future of film-making?
links.excerpt: It should have taken years, but Ash Koosha made a drama about Iran’s anti-government protests in weeks – and now it’s the first AI-made movie to screen at a major film festival. It could transform indie film-making, claims the director Next week a breakthrough 75-minute drama about the brutal crackdown in Iran on anti-government protesters in January will premiere at the Tribeca film festival in New York. It is called Dreams of Violets</a
links.imageAlt: ‘The CGI would have cost millions. I spent $2,000.’ Is Dreams of Violets AI slop – or the future of film-making?

stories.title: Anthropic says the world should have option to ‘pause’ on AI
stories.summary:
Anthropic has proposed a global temporary pause on artificial intelligence development to facilitate discussions regarding the risks associated with advanced systems. The US-based company announced plans to convene policymakers for these critical safety dialogues.

The proposal was outlined in a recent publication detailing the progress of its Claude model. This release serves to highlight both the capabilities of their current technology and the potential dangers inherent in further advancement.

stories.title: The Verge Weekend Questionnaire
stories.summary:
The Verge has launched its Weekend Questionnaire, a recurring feature designed to offer rapid-fire insights into the personal habits and preferences of notable figures in technology and entertainment. Serving as a spiritual successor to the earlier Five Minutes on the Verge segment, the series invites a different guest each Saturday to answer a curated menu of quick questions.

The format aims to uncover details that readers might otherwise miss, such as the most indispensable apps used by favorite musicians or the specific strategies tech CEOs employ to maintain focus. By shifting away from traditional long-form interviews, this approach provides a more casual and immediate glimpse into the daily routines and digital tools of industry leaders.

links.headline: The Verge Weekend Questionnaire
links.excerpt: Have you ever wondered what the most indispensable app is for your favorite musician or how the world’s tech CEOs stay focused? Well, that’s the sort of thing we aim to uncover in our Verge Weekend Questionnaire. Think of it as a spiritual successor to Five Minutes on the Verge . stories.title: No hospital arrest for Curlee Discaya, says lawyer
stories.summary:
Government contractor Pacifico “Curlee” Discaya was arrested at the Senate on Monday, marking his turnover to police after nine months of detention. The arrest stems from a non-bailable malversation case linked to an alleged P53.9-million flood control project in Calumpit, Bulacan.

The Bulacan Regional Trial Court Branch 20 issued the warrant due to accusations regarding the falsification of public funds or property. Despite the court order, Discaya’s lawyer stated that he will not seek hospital arrest while still recovering from shoulder surgery.

stories.title: Phivolcs: Tsunami warning out in offshore M7.8 earthquake in Sarangani
stories.summary:
State seismologists in the Philippines issued a tsunami warning on Monday morning following a powerful magnitude 7.8 earthquake that struck off the coast of Sarangani province.

The Philippine Institute of Volcanology and Seismology (Phivolcs) reported that the quake occurred at 7:37 a.m. with a depth of just 10 kilometers, prompting warnings for wave heights exceeding one meter above normal tides.

According to live updates from Rappler, the significant seismic event rocked various parts of Mindanao on June 8, 2026. Authorities continue to monitor the situation as aftershocks and potential impacts are assessed across the affected regions.

links.headline: Phivolcs: Tsunami warning out in offshore M7.8 earthquake in Sarangani
links.excerpt: [Updated June 8, 2026, 9:18 a.m.] MANILA, Philippines — State seismologists on Monday issued a tsunami warning with wave height of more than one meter above the normal tides after a magnitude 7.8 earthquake struck off the shores of Saragani province. stories.title: Gatchalian on Senate row: ‘I will talk to everyone’
stories.summary:
Senator Sherwin Gatchalian has expressed openness to dialogue with Senator Alan Peter Cayetano’s bloc in an effort to resolve a leadership impasse that has divided the Philippine Senate. The internal conflict recently stalled legislative work just before the upper chamber scheduled an adjournment lasting nearly two months.

The House of Representatives has also urged the Senate to address the deadlock, highlighting the broader impact on national governance. Meanwhile, separate investigations into flood control have emerged within the chamber.

A probe led by Senator Erwin Tulfo was conducted by his version of the blue ribbon committee. This hearing took place shortly after Cayetano’s bloc held its own similar investigation on the same issue, illustrating competing approaches to oversight.

links.headline: Gatchalian on Senate row: ‘I will talk to everyone’
links.excerpt: MANILA, Philippines — Senator Sherwin Gatchalian on Sunday said he is open to a dialogue with Sen. Alan Peter Cayetano’s bloc to settle the leadership impasse, which has divided the upper chamber and stalled legislative work last week ahead of an adjournment of almost two months. The House of Representatives also urged the Senate to

stories.title: Erwin Tulfo-led blue ribbon panel resumes flood control probe
stories.summary:
The Senate blue ribbon committee, chaired by Senator Erwin Tulfo, has resumed its investigation into alleged irregularities in flood control projects. The probe aims to uncover potential misuse of funds and corruption within the sector.

Several prominent figures have been invited to testify as resource persons during the hearings. Former Speaker Martin Romualdez is among those called to provide information regarding the ongoing inquiry.

Additionally, four members of the House of Representatives were summoned to participate in the proceedings. These include Leila de Lima, Jose Alvarez, Jullienne Baronda, and a representative from Lanao del Sur.

The investigation has gained further momentum with new testimonies emerging. Attorney Levito Baligod confirmed that 14 more former employees of ex-Congressman Zaldy Co are prepared to face the Senate.

links.headline: 14 more ex-employees of Zaldy Co ready to face Senate — Baligod
links.excerpt: MANILA, Philippines — Another 14 supposed former employees of ex-Congressman Zaldy Co are ready to face the Senate investigation, Atty. Levito Baligod confirmed on Monday. This is on top of the first 18 who claimed to be bagmen of Co, and allegedly delivered cash-filled luggages to several government officials in relation to the flood control

stories.title: Alan Cayetano camp seeks interim admin setup with new Senate majority
stories.summary:
The camp of Senator Alan Peter Cayetano, who maintains he remains the Senate president, has proposed interim administrative arrangements to the new Senate majority group. This group is currently led by Senate President Pro Tempore Sherwin Gatchalian following a shift in leadership dynamics.

One specific arrangement under consideration involves the adoption of dual-signatory or joint certification protocols for important documents. This measure would require signatures from both current officials and those still asserting authority, aiming to bridge the administrative gap during the transition period.

links.headline: Alan Cayetano camp seeks interim admin setup with new Senate majority
links.excerpt: MANILA, Philippines— The camp of Sen. Alan Peter Cayetano, who claims he’s still the Senate president, is proposing possible interim administrative arrangements to the new majority group now led by Senate President Pro Tempore Sherwin Gatchalian. One possible arrangement is the adoption of a dual-signatory or joint certification by current officials and those still asserting authority on important documents such

stories.title: Who Are the Israeli Hostages Released by Hamas?
stories.summary:
Four Israeli soldiers, all women, were released from the Gaza Strip as part of a fragile cease-fire agreement with Hamas. This marks the second group of hostages to be freed under the current deal.

The release has drawn significant attention in Israel, where citizens held their breath during the operation. The event highlights the ongoing tensions and humanitarian concerns surrounding the conflict.

links.headline: Who Are the Israeli Hostages Released by Hamas?
links.excerpt: Israelis held their breath as four soldiers, all women, were released from the Gaza Strip, the second group to be freed as part of a fragile cease-fire agreement with Hamas.
links.imageAlt: Who Are the Israeli Hostages Released by Hamas?

stories.title: GM’s electric future depends on a new battery — and this facility
stories.summary:
General Motors is accelerating its electric vehicle strategy by aiming to deploy advanced battery technology up to a year ahead of schedule. This accelerated timeline is central to the automaker's goal of significantly reducing EV prices for consumers.

A specific manufacturing facility has emerged as critical infrastructure in achieving this objective. The plant plays a pivotal role in producing the new battery components necessary to support GM's expanded electric lineup.

links.headline: GM’s electric future depends on a new battery — and this facility
links.excerpt: GM wants to slash EV prices by deploying new battery tech up to a year earlier than planned. This building is key to making that happen.

stories.title: Amazon expands ultra-fast deliveries in UK and adds same-day fruit and veg
stories.summary:
Amazon is expanding its ultra-fast delivery services across the UK, introducing same-day fruit and vegetable options alongside existing fast-track logistics. The company announced that Amazon Now will extend deliveries in 30 minutes or less to Manchester and Birmingham, building on its current operational footprint.

In addition to rapid non-grocery items, the retailer is launching a fresh grocery service starting in London. This move follows the recent closure of its standalone Whole Foods Market stores in the region, signaling a strategic shift toward integrated digital fulfillment rather than physical retail locations for perishable goods.

links.headline: Amazon expands ultra-fast deliveries in UK and adds same-day fruit and veg
links.excerpt: Deliveries in 30 minutes or less coming to Manchester and Birmingham and fresh groceries service to start in London Amazon is expanding fast-track deliveries in the UK, including adding fresh fruit and vegetables to same-day services, after closing its standalone grocery stores . stories.title: Jeju Air Crash Probe Focuses On Engine Damage After Bird Strike
stories.summary:
Investigators are examining engine damage following a bird strike as the primary focus of the Jeju Air crash probe. The incident has prompted a detailed review of the mechanical impact on the aircraft's propulsion systems.

According to individuals familiar with the ongoing investigation, the actions taken by the pilots immediately after the bird strike are also under early scrutiny. This dual focus aims to determine whether mechanical failure or human response played a more significant role in the outcome.

links.headline: Jeju Air Crash Probe Focuses On Engine Damage After Bird Strike
links.excerpt: Pilots’ actions after that strike are an early focus of the investigation, according to people familiar with the probe.
links.imageAlt: Jeju Air Crash Probe Focuses On Engine Damage After Bird Strike

stories.title: Hamas Took More Than 200 Hostages From Israel. Here's What We Know.
stories.summary:
Hamas abducted more than 200 hostages from Israel during the October 7 attacks, according to reports. The Wall Street Journal outlines what is currently known about the captives and their status.

Israel states that 91 of these individuals remain in captivity as of now. Among those held are the bodies of at least 34 hostages who have died while being detained by Hamas.

links.headline: Hamas Took More Than 200 Hostages From Israel. Here's What We Know.
links.excerpt: Israel says 91 hostages abducted on Oct. 7 remain in captivity, including the bodies of at least 34.
links.imageAlt: Hamas Took More Than 200 Hostages From Israel. Here's What We Know.
